claim: For all integers n, n^3 - n is divisible by 6.
For all integers n, n^5 - n is divisible by 30.
For all integers n, n^7 - n is divisible by 42.
For all integers n, n^11 - n is divisible by 66.
For all integers n, n^13 - n is divisible by 2730.
For all integers n, n^17 - n is divisible by 510.
For all integers n, n^19 - n is divisible by 798.
For all integers n, n^23 - n is divisible by 138.
For all odd integers n, n^3 - n is divisible by 24.
For all integers n congruent to 3 modulo 6, n^3 - n is divisible by 24.
For all integers n congruent to 1 modulo 4, n^3 - n is divisible by 24.
For all integers n congruent to 3 modulo 4, n^3 - n is divisible by 24.
For all integers n coprime to 6, n^3 - n is divisible by 24.
For all integers n congruent to 1 or 11 modulo 12, n^3 - n is divisible by 24.
For all integers n coprime to 30, n^5 - n is divisible by 120.
For all integers n coprime to 30, n^5 - n is divisible by 240.
For all odd integers n coprime to 3, gcd(n^3 - n, 24) is 24.
For all odd integers n coprime to 15, gcd(n^5 - n, 120) is 120.
For all odd integers n coprime to 21, gcd(n^7 - n, 504) is 504.
For all integers n with absolute value greater than 3, the Euler totient of n^3 - n is divisible by 4.
For all integers n with absolute value greater than 4, the Euler totient of n^3 - n is divisible by 8.
For all integers n with absolute value greater than 5, the Euler totient of n^3 - n is divisible by 16.
For all integers n with absolute value greater than 5, the Euler totient of n^3 - n is divisible by 24.
For all integers n with absolute value greater than 3, the divisor count tau of n^3 - n is at least 8.
For all integers n with absolute value greater than 4, the divisor count tau of n^3 - n is at least 12.
For all integers n with absolute value greater than 2, the divisor count tau of n^5 - n is at least 16.
For all even integers n with absolute value greater than 2, the sum of divisors sigma of n^3 - n is divisible by 24.
For all even integers n with absolute value greater than 4 and divisible by 3, the sum of divisors sigma of n^3 - n is divisible by 48.
For all even integers n with absolute value greater than 10 and divisible by 6, the sum of divisors sigma of n^3 - n is divisible by 96.
For all prime values of n greater than 3, n^2 - 1 is divisible by 24.
For all prime values of n greater than 3, n^2 - 1 is divisible by 12.
For all prime values of n greater than 3, n^2 - 1 is divisible by 8.
For all prime values of n greater than 5, n^4 - 1 is divisible by 240.
For all prime values of n greater than 5, n^4 - 1 is divisible by 80.
For all prime values of n greater than 5, n^4 - 1 is divisible by 48.
For all even integers n not equal to -1, 0, or 1, the 2-adic valuation of n^3 - n is at least 1.
For all odd integers n not equal to -1, 0, or 1, the 2-adic valuation of n^3 - n is at least 3.
For all integers n congruent to 3 modulo 4 (except -1, 0, 1), the 2-adic valuation of n^3 - n is at least 3.
For all integers n congruent to 7 modulo 8 (except -1, 0, 1), the 2-adic valuation of n^3 - n is at least 4.
For all integers n congruent to 15 modulo 16 (except -1, 0, 1), the 2-adic valuation of n^3 - n is at least 5.
For all integers n not equal to -1, 0, or 1, the 3-adic valuation of n^3 - n is at least 1.
For all integers n congruent to 8 modulo 9 (except -1, 0, 1), the 3-adic valuation of n^3 - n is at least 2.
For all integers n congruent to 26 modulo 27 (except -1, 0, 1), the 3-adic valuation of n^3 - n is at least 3.
For all positive integers n, the sum from k=1 to n of k^3 - k is divisible by 6.
predicate: (n < 1) or (sum(k3 - k for k in range(1, n+1)) % 6 == 0)

claim: For all positive integers n, twelve times the sum from k=1 to n of k^3 - k is divisible by 72.
predicate: (n < 1) or ((12 * sum(k3 - k for k in range(1, n+1))) % 72 == 0)

claim: For all odd integers n, n^6 - n^2 is divisible by 48.
For all odd integers n not divisible by 5, n^6 - n^2 is divisible by 240.
For all integers n congruent to 1 or 9 modulo 10, n^6 - n^2 is divisible by 480.
For all integers n, n^9 - n is divisible by 30.
For all odd integers n not divisible by 5, n^9 - n is divisible by 480.
For all integers n congruent to 3 modulo 8 and coprime to 5, n^9 - n is divisible by 960.
For all integers n, the gcd of n^3 - n and (n+1)^3 - (n+1) is divisible by n*(n+1).
For all integers n congruent to 1 modulo 3, the gcd of n^3 - n and (n+1)^3 - (n+1) is equal to 3|n*(n+1)|.
